definition: A *testament* is a witnessed writing — first a legal will, then, in Christian usage, either half of Scripture. To call the Bible's two parts the Old and New Testaments is to call them witnessed covenants between God and humanity, sworn and set down rather than merely told.
history: From Latin *testamentum*, 'a will', built on *testis*, 'a witness' — the same root as *testify*. The turn is legal before it is scriptural: a testament is a covenant made valid by witnesses, and the Church borrowed the word to name God's two covenants, the New sealed, in the ritual formula, by 'my blood of the new testament'. The witnessing sense runs everywhere through learned magic, whose oaths, adjurations, and sworn books (the *Liber Iuratus*) bind spirits and readers alike by testimony.

def_sources.label: Richard Kieckhefer, Magic in the Middle Ages
Peterson & Harms, Book of Magic with Instructions for Invoking Spirits

etymology.origin: Latin testamentum 'a will', from testis 'a witness'
etymology.note: The Old and New 'Testaments' are 'witnessed covenants'. Same testis as testify — and, by an old Roman oath-custom, testicle.
etymology.hook: Why the Bible is named after a legal will.

flavor_snippets.html:
"The Reito Massacre was a <mark>testament</mark> to the kami's unstoppable power. The human defenders might as well have been moths battling a forest fire."
—*Great Battles of Kamigawa*

"It's a <mark>testament</mark> to the forests' devastation that giant spiders now make their homes amid Dominaria's barren spires."
—Aznaph, greenseeker
For wisdom's sake, it was built high to gaze on all things. For glory's sake, it was built high as a <mark>testament</mark> of power. For strength's sake, it was built high to repel all attacks.
Neither its appearance nor its temperature varies as the years pass, an eternal <mark>testament</mark> to the forces that shaped Dominaria.

flavor_snippets.card: Midgar, City of Mako // Reactor Raid
flavor_snippets.html: A triumph of technology and <mark>testament</mark> to man's potential.

"Ixalli's fertile rays enliven us all. Each of us is a <mark>testament</mark> to the sun's strength and love."
—Chitlati, Lightning Rider

"An ancient court mage unified the houses into guilds to serve Zhalfir above their own interests. Even during the civil war, the guilds stood together, a <mark>testament</mark> to the wisdom of Teferi."
—Afari, *Tales*

"The battle at Ganzan Pass was a <mark>testament</mark> to human endurance. The untrained and frightened group escaped three legions of kami, leaving more than their share of slain foes in their wake."
—*Great Battles of Kamigawa*
Volcanic ash-winds batter it, climbing vines overwhelm it, dragonfire roasts it, and yet it still stands as a <mark>testament</mark> to a forgotten world.
Liliana viewed the attention of the angels as a <mark>testament</mark> to her power. Even so, they were an annoyance.
